title: CPR — Adult (Age 12+)

steps.instruction: Check for responsiveness. Tap shoulders firmly and shout 'Are you okay?'
steps.tip: If no response, shout for help. Send someone to call emergency services and get an AED.

steps.instruction: Place the person on their back on a firm, flat surface.
steps.tip: If you suspect spinal injury, minimize movement. Roll them as a unit.

steps.instruction: Open the airway. Tilt the head back and lift the chin up.
steps.tip: Place one hand on the forehead and two fingers under the chin. Look, listen, and feel for breathing for no more than 10 seconds.

steps.instruction: Begin chest compressions. Place the heel of one hand on the center of the chest, between the nipples. Place the other hand on top and interlock fingers.
steps.tip: Keep your arms straight and shoulders directly over your hands.

steps.instruction: Push hard and fast: compress at least 2 inches deep at a rate of 100-120 compressions per minute. Give 30 compressions.
steps.tip: Push to the beat of 'Stayin' Alive' by the Bee Gees. Allow full chest recoil between compressions.

steps.instruction: Give 2 rescue breaths. Tilt head back, pinch nose shut, create seal over mouth, and blow for 1 second each. Watch for chest rise.
steps.tip: If you're untrained or uncomfortable, skip rescue breaths and do compression-only CPR.

steps.instruction: Continue cycles of 30 compressions and 2 breaths. Do NOT stop until help arrives, an AED is available, or the person starts breathing.
steps.tip: If another person is available, switch compressors every 2 minutes to avoid fatigue. Tired compressions are ineffective.

when_to_stop: AED is attached and advises to stop
You become physically unable to continue
do_not: Do NOT give up — brain damage begins after 4 minutes without oxygen
Do NOT check for pulse if you are untrained
Do NOT move the person unless they are in danger
